title: Ensure EBS volume encryption is enabled in all regions

input_row.title: Ensure EBS volume encryption is enabled in all regions
input_row.description: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) supports encryption at rest when using the Elastic Block Store (EBS) service. While disabled by default, forcing encryption at EBS volume creation is supported.
input_row.rationale: Encrypting data at rest reduces the likelihood of unintentional exposure and can nullify the impact of disclosure if the encryption remains unbroken.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon EC2 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. Under Account attributes, click Data Protection and Security. 3. Under EBS encryption, verify that Always encrypt new EBS volumes displays Enabled. 4. Repeat for each region in use. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region. From Command Line: 1. Run the following command: aws --region <region> ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default 2. Verify that "EbsEncryptionByDefault": true is displayed. 3. Repeat for each region in use. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon EC2 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. Under Account attributes, click Data protection and security. 3. Under EBS encryption, Click Manage. 4. Check the Enable box to default encryption. 5. Click Update EBS encryption. 6. Repeat for each region in which EBS volume encryption is not enabled by default. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region. From Command Line: 1. Run the following command: aws --region <region> ec2 enable-ebs-encryption-by-default 2. Verify that "EbsEncryptionByDefault": true is displayed. 3. Repeat for each region in which EBS volume encryption is not enabled by default. Note: EBS volume encryption is configured per region.
input_row.impact: Losing access to or removing the KMS key used by the EBS volumes will result in the inability to access the volumes.
input_row.default_value: By default, EBS volume encryption is disabled. It must be manually enabled per region; otherwise, new EBS volumes are created unencrypted.
input_row.additional_information: Default EBS volume encryption only applies to newly created EBS volumes; existing EBS volumes are not converted automatically.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be fully verified via AWS APIs/boto3/AWS CLI commands, as outlined in the provided audit steps. EBS encryption status can be queried directly.
- There are defined boto3 methods and AWS CLI commands to check whether EBS encryption by default is enabled in regions.
- The description and remediation give clear instructions on assessing and configuring the property using AWS CLI, confirming that the feature can be managed programmatically.
  
**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ec2_ebs_encryption_default_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the AWS CLI command `aws ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default --region <region>` to verify whether EBS volume encryption is enabled by default.
- Use the boto3 SDK method `ec2.get_ebs_encryption_by_default()` to check the same property for each region.
- Enumerate all regions by calling `ec2.describe_regions()` to check EBS encryption in each one.
- Expected configuration: The output should include `"EbsEncryptionByDefault": true` for compliance.
- Pass/Fail criteria: 
  - Pass: If for each checked region, the returned value indicates that EBS encryption by default is enabled (`"EbsEncryptionByDefault": true`).
  - Fail: If any region returns `"EbsEncryptionByDefault": false`. 

This approach enables a comprehensive and automated validation of EBS volume encryption settings across all AWS regions, ensuring compliance with CIS control 6.1.1 effectively.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively checked using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands.
- The AWS CLI command `aws ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default --region <region>` and the boto3 method `ec2.get_ebs_encryption_by_default()` are appropriate for verifying EBS encryption settings.
- The approach leverages AWS SDK capabilities to automate the compliance check across all regions, which aligns well with security best practices.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The boto3 method and AWS CLI command mentioned are valid and correctly used for the intended purpose.
- The method `ec2.describe_regions()` is correctly used to enumerate all regions, ensuring comprehensive coverage.
- The program name `aws_ec2_ebs_encryption_default_enabled` is descriptive and follows the naming convention, although it could be slightly more specific by including "by_default" to emphasize the default setting check.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `aws_ec2_ebs_encryption_by_default_enabled` to explicitly indicate that the check is for the default encryption setting.
- Consider adding error handling in the automation script to manage potential API call failures or permission issues.
- It might be beneficial to include a brief explanation of how to remediate if a region is found non-compliant, such as enabling EBS encryption by default using the AWS CLI command `aws ec2 enable-ebs-encryption-by-default --region <region>`.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and utilizes appropriate AWS services and methods. The technical details are accurate, and the automation approach is robust.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `aws_ec2_ebs_encryption_by_default_enabled` for clarity and specificity.
- Otherwise, keep the assessment as-is, as the automated approach is valid and effectively ensures compliance with the control.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the control can be effectively verified using AWS APIs, specifically through AWS CLI and boto3 methods. The AWS CLI command `aws ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default --region <region>` and the boto3 method `ec2.get_ebs_encryption_by_default()` allow for checking the encryption status across all regions programmatically. This aligns with the control's requirements and ensures comprehensive compliance checks.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_ec2_ebs_encryption_by_default_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** EC2 EBS Volumes
- **API/CLI Method:** boto3 `ec2.get_ebs_encryption_by_default()`, AWS CLI `aws ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default --region <region>`
- **Properties to Check:** `"EbsEncryptionByDefault"`
- **Pass Criteria:** `"EbsEncryptionByDefault": true` for all regions
- **Fail Criteria:** Any region returns `"EbsEncryptionByDefault": false`

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The second opinion suggested a slight improvement in the program name for clarity, which has been adopted.
